{"key_event":"Swadeshi mobilization and colonial repression","reasoning":"Civil society grew from elite petitioning toward broader nationalist mobilization through Swadeshi boycotts, vernacular press, associations, and indigenous institutions, though the Congress split and communal separate electorates limited unified pressure. The colonial state remained very strong and gained administrative, surveillance, coercive, and wartime capacities, even while concessions such as Bengal’s annulment showed it was not unchecked.","society_change":0.8,"society_power":5.0,"state_change":0.4,"state_power":8.4}
